summary:
The child shows symptoms indicative of moderate dehydration such as sunken eyes and delayed skin turgor. Management should focus on oral hydration therapy using small, frequent amounts while monitoring closely; maintenance feedings continue post-improvement due to potential continued fluid loss. Summary:

A moderately dehydrated young boy requires treatment primarily through oral rehydration solutions administered frequently along with continuous dietary intake under medical supervision until fully recovered from initial deficits without immediate need for intravenous intervention based solely on current presentation. Key aspects include avoiding large volumes initially which could induce nausea/vomiting instead opting smaller boluses more often coupled with nutritional support throughout recovery phase.
